retroactive_fill_note: This scan covers April 25, 2026 (3-day lookback: April 22-25). It was generated retroactively on April 24 actual wall-clock date. All evidence is constrained to events on or before April 25, 2026.
assessor_note: Compact handoff — T1 priority entities plus T2/T3. Full evidence, rationales, and source URLs are in 2026-04-25.json. Key new material vs April 24: (1) Musk drops fraud claims April 25 — trial scope changed; (2) Danaher $172.5M settlement — new T1; (3) General Mills WARN Act + racial class-action — new T1; (4) Eli Lilly SCOTUS FCA petition — new T1; (5) Booz Allen Treasury cancellation — first pipeline capture; (6) Finland/Sweden elevated from T2 to T1 on independent event evidence.
critical_pre_assessment_tasks: RETRIEVE UNMISS vote outcome at press.un.org/en before scoring South Sudan. Vote expected before April 30 — 5 days remain. If vote has occurred, score based on renewal terms. If not, maintain deferral.
RETRIEVE Musk v. Altman trial Day 1 (April 27) proceedings before finalizing OpenAI score. Fraud claims withdrawn April 25 — two remaining claims: unjust enrichment + breach of charitable trust.

RECONCILE Anthropic rotation-state composite (90.9) with applied score (61.6). Update rotation-state.json: anthropic.composite → 61.6. This is the fourth consecutive scan flagging this — action overdue.
RECONCILE TIAA composite: rotation-state (92.5) vs. fortune-500.json raw data. Read site/src/data/indexes/fortune-500.json before scoring TIAA.
VERIFY Luxembourg and Norway index file updates: check countries.json for whether April 22-24 corrections have been written to the live index files.
CONFIRM Myanmar date-drift: earthquake was March 28, 2025 (not April 24, 2026). Remove acute-earthquake flag from any scoring context. Myanmar's junta atrocity evidence remains valid for assessment.
DO NOT propose scoring changes for Interpublic Group — entity-existence decision pending founder signoff.

t1_queue.name: OpenAI
t1_queue.key_trigger: Musk drops fraud claims April 25 — trial narrowed to unjust enrichment + breach of charitable trust; jury selection April 27
t1_queue.assessor_action: Score fraud-claim withdrawal as governance event: charitable-trust breach claim directly addresses founding mission abandonment. Retrieve trial Day 1 proceedings before finalizing. Note: fraud withdrawal narrows, not eliminates, the INT dimension exposure.

t1_queue.name: Danaher
t1_queue.key_trigger: $172.5M shareholder class-action settlement April 23 — largest DC federal court securities settlement since 1996; Gen-2 cluster (76.4, rank 22 F500)
t1_queue.assessor_action: First baseline assessment. Score securities fraud settlement (misleading statements re: post-pandemic bioprocessing demand) against INT/ACC. Gen-2 forensic audit: verify 76.4 subdimension profile. Assess diagnostics/life sciences mission for genuine SYS positive values.

t1_queue.name: South Sudan
t1_queue.key_trigger: UNMISS mandate expires April 30 — 5 days; vote not yet held; SSPDF-SPLA-IO hostilities at 2018-peak scale
t1_queue.assessor_action: Check press.un.org for UNMISS vote result before scoring. If vote held: score renewal terms. If not: maintain deferral with urgency note (5 days to expiry).

t1_queue.name: General Mills
t1_queue.key_trigger: WARN Act violation investigation (163 workers, April 7) + racial harassment class-action re-instated April 22; Gen-2 cluster
t1_queue.assessor_action: First baseline. Score WARN Act violation risk (EMP), racial class-action (EQU), and Gen-2 forensic audit. Two independent event triggers make this a high-confidence assessment target.

t1_queue.name: xAI/Grok
t1_queue.key_trigger: SpaceX S-1 IPO filing warns investors: Grok investigations may cause market bans — harm cluster now company-admitted material risk in SEC filing
t1_queue.assessor_action: Score IPO prospectus risk disclosure as evidence-quality upgrade: harm now company-admitted. Note floor dynamics (2.2). Document SEC/DOJ investigation status. Composite is at or near minimum; document escalation for future methodology review.

t1_queue.name: Finland
t1_queue.key_trigger: Emergency asylum law extended through Dec 2026; April 16 deportation-speed + entry-ban reforms submitted to Parliament; ceiling artifact at 100
t1_queue.assessor_action: Countries ceiling Night 5 anchor. Assess together with Denmark (both composite-100). Emergency asylum law extension is independent event evidence. Expect ceiling correction similar to Iceland (-12.5) or Iceland/Luxembourg average.

t1_queue.name: Sweden
t1_queue.key_trigger: Government proposed vague 'good conduct' Aliens Act residence permit requirement — discriminatory two-tier standard for migrants; ceiling artifact at 97.2
t1_queue.assessor_action: Promoted from T2. Independent event evidence: Aliens Act good-conduct proposal creates vague discriminatory standard. Countries ceiling methodology audit + event scoring. Amnesty April 2026 annual report is supplementary evidence source.

t1_queue.name: Booz Allen Hamilton
t1_queue.key_trigger: Treasury cancelled all 31 contracts ($21M) January 2026 — IRS tax-record breach by Booz Allen employee (406K taxpayers affected); stock fell 10%+
t1_queue.assessor_action: First pipeline capture — never previously flagged. Score Treasury contract cancellation as INT/ACC governance failure. Assess scope of insider-threat controls gap. Note April 25 EO 14398 exposure as secondary context.

t1_queue.name: Eli Lilly
t1_queue.key_trigger: Supreme Court petition March 21 to eliminate FCA whistleblower (qui tam) provisions as unconstitutional; Gen-2 cluster; ICCR HRDD gap
t1_queue.assessor_action: First baseline. Score SCOTUS FCA petition (intent to eliminate accountability mechanism) as INT/ACC failure regardless of outcome. Gen-2 forensic audit for 76.4. Assess GLP-1 access vs insulin continuity against healthcare mission.

t1_queue.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
t1_queue.key_trigger: MONUSCO chief Swan April 25 briefing: conditions for effective ceasefire monitoring not met; ongoing fighting South Kivu; 6.4M displaced
t1_queue.assessor_action: Reassessment check against April 22 confirmation (4.4). Swan's conditions signal ineffective protection posture. Ceasefire violations ongoing. Likely reaffirmation at 4.4 unless evidence of improvement.

t1_queue.name: Israel
t1_queue.key_trigger: IRC April 22 report: aid down 37% months 3-6 of ceasefire; 150K children acute malnutrition; composite 3.4pts from critical boundary
t1_queue.assessor_action: Score IRC deterioration evidence against 23.4 composite. Apply band-change protocol if evidence supports moving below 20 (critical boundary). 150K acute malnutrition cases is a direct, quantified welfare metric.

t1_queue.name: Palantir AI
t1_queue.key_trigger: Congressional deadline expired April 24 (no confirmed response); EFF April 25 analysis documents gap between stated human rights policy and ICE surveillance practice
t1_queue.assessor_action: Score EFF INT gap analysis as evidence quality upgrade. Congressional non-response by ICE/DHS is ACC failure. Score 22-point manifesto as values-gap evidence for INT subdimension I3 (consistency between stated values and verified practices).

t1_queue.name: Cummins
t1_queue.key_trigger: $1.675B EPA defeat-device settlement (Jan 2024) — largest Clean Air Act civil penalty in history; Gen-2 cluster (76.4, rank 20 F500)
t1_queue.assessor_action: First baseline. Score defeat-device settlement as deliberate environmental fraud affecting overburdened communities. Gen-2 forensic audit: 76.4 uniform profile is inconsistent with documented emissions cheating at scale. Assess ongoing 2026 recall compliance obligations.

t1_queue.name: United States
t1_queue.key_trigger: EO 14398 effective today April 25; Labor Secretary Chavez-DeRemer resigned April 20 (third consecutive Cabinet misconduct resignation); Amnesty 'predatory world order' framing
t1_queue.assessor_action: Score EO 14398 as domestic equity event (anti-DEI enforcement creates disparate impact). Score Chavez-DeRemer resignation as executive branch institutional failure. Last assessed April 17 — update with April 22-25 material.

t1_queue.assessor_action: Countries ceiling methodology audit. No independent event evidence in window — elevated for methodological consistency alongside Finland. Use Amnesty April 2026 annual report as primary evidence source. Assess together with Finland in same session.

gen2_sprint_status.assessor_note: All 6 remaining Gen-2 cluster entities at 76.4 now have documented compassion-relevant evidence independent of the methodology pattern. The sprint can be completed in one structured batch session using the Becton Dickinson forensic anchor (-27.3) as calibration reference. Highest-priority batch order: Danaher (securities settlement, T1) → General Mills (WARN Act + racial class-action, T1) → Cummins (defeat device, T1) → Eli Lilly (SCOTUS FCA petition, T1) → U.S. Bancorp (CFPB + unauthorized accounts, T2) → American Express (DOJ deceptive marketing, T2).
